content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic rewards (from ICM/RND frameworks) can be embedded as a hierarchical pruning signal inside hybrid planning-learning systems to dynamically prioritize which exploratory memory traces get retained for long-horizon planning, rather than just storing all active learning candidate experiences. This creates a self-organizing memory buffer that automatically grows around high-uncertainty, high-learning-value states that accelerate both planning convergence and out-of-distribution generalization.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
